title: In vitro digestion of DNA with Cas9 Nuclease, S. pyogenes (M0386)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eahsv","text":"Cas9 Nuclease, S. pyogenes, (Cas9) is a double-stranded DNA endonuclease that is guided to its target by sequence complementarity of a small RNA loaded into the protein. This protocol describes how to digest double-stranded DNA in vitro using Cas9 and a single guide RNA (sgRNA).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":15,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":228,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

(2013) Science 339 (6121): 823-826.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2cmv7","text":"We strongly recommend wearing gloves and using nuclease-free tubes and reagents to avoid RNase contamination. Further recommendations for avoiding ribonuclease contamination can be found here.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":187,"length":4}],"data":{}},{"key":"e52k2","text":"Reactions are typically 30 μl but can be scaled up as needed. Reactions should be assembled in nuclease-free microfuge tubes or PCR strip tubes.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d5go6","text":"It is essential to keep the molar ratio of Cas9 and sgRNA per target site at 10:10:1 or higher to obtain the best cleavage efficiency. A calculator can be found here.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":161,"length":4}],"data":{}},{"key":"6u298","text":"If planning to use steps: Assemble the reaction at Room temperature in the following order: A B 1 COMPONENT VOLUME (for 30 µl reaction) 2 Nuclease-free water 20 µl 3 NEBuffer 3.1 3 µl 4 300 nM sgRNA 3 µl (30 nM final) 5 1 µM Cas9 Nuclease, S.pyogenes (M0386S) 1 µl (~30 nM final) 6 Reaction volume 27 µl *The sgRNA and nuclease-free water are not included.
Add 3 µL, 30 nM substrate DNA (3 nM final).
Mix thoroughly and pulse-spin in a microfuge.
Add 1 µL, Proteinase K to each sample. Mix thoroughly and pulse-spin in a microfuge.
Prepare 300 nanomolar (nM), sgRNA by diluting the stock with nuclease-free water On ice .
Prepare 30 nanomolar (nM), substrate DNA with a single target sequence by diluting the stock with nuclease-free water On ice .
creator: New England   Biolabs
